nonGoals: Do NOT change scorer.go — it already imports language_tiers and uses TierForLanguage/TierGeneric
Do NOT add new analyzers or evidence collectors
Do NOT break the TierGeneric fallback contract (unknown language → TierGeneric)
objective: Replace the existing 2-tier enum {TierGeneric, TierFull} with a 5-tier iota enum {TierNone, TierGeneric, TierLint, TierParse, TierFull}, add a String() method, define a static map[string]Tier registry covering all 22 language IDs, and update TierForLanguage(lang) to return the mapped tier or TierGeneric for unknowns.
planText: In internal/language_tiers/language_tiers.go: (1) Redefine Tier as an iota enum: TierNone=0, TierGeneric, TierLint, TierParse, TierFull. (2) Add a String() method returning the human-readable tier name. (3) Define `var languageTiers = map[string]Tier{…}` with all 22 language IDs: go/ts/js/py/rs → TierFull; rb/java/kt/sh/c/cpp/cs/swift/php/elixir/erlang/scala/lua/r/zig/sql/proto → TierGeneric. (4) Update TierForLanguage(lang string) Tier to look up the registry and return TierGeneric when the key is missing. Ensure TierGeneric stays at iota value 1 (backward-compatible with existing scorer.go usage that compares against TierGeneric).

nonGoals: Do NOT modify tier definitions here — tests only
Do NOT add integration or end-to-end tests
Do NOT depend on any files outside discovery and language_tiers packages
objective: Add three tests: (a) TestTierRegistry_AllKnownLanguagesHaveTiers — imports discovery.KnownLanguages() and asserts every ID returns a non-zero, explicitly-mapped tier; (b) TestTierRegistry_UnknownLanguageReturnsGeneric — asserts TierForLanguage("nonsense") == TierGeneric; (c) TestTierRegistry_SwiftIsGeneric — regression pin asserting TierForLanguage("swift") == TierGeneric.
planText: In internal/language_tiers/language_tiers_test.go, replace or augment the existing test file with three test functions. Test (a) calls discovery.KnownLanguages(), iterates every returned ID, and asserts TierForLanguage(id) != TierNone — this is the CI gate: adding a new extension to certifiableExts without a corresponding tier entry will fail. Test (b) asserts the unknown fallback returns TierGeneric. Test (c) asserts the explicit Swift-to-Generic mapping as a regression pin. Run `go test ./... -count=1` to confirm all pass.
